1.title: The vision of Isaiah
1.text: Isaiah prophesied in Judah under four kings: Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah.

1.question: How does Isaiah describe the rebellious people's condition?
options: The whole head sick and the whole heart faint

1.question: To whom does the Lord liken the rulers and people of Judah?
options: To the rulers of Sodom and people of Gomorrah

1.question: Why does the Lord hide His eyes and not hear their prayers?
options: Their hands are full of blood

1.question: According to 1:18, though your sins be as scarlet, they shall become?
What will the city be called after it is purified?
options: The city of righteousness, the faithful city

1.question: According to 1:27, with what shall Zion be redeemed?

2.title: The Lord's mountain
2.text: The last-days vision (2:2-4) foretells peace among nations and the law going forth from Zion.

2.question: Into what will the nations beat their swords?
Besides silver and gold, with what was the land of Judah full?
Against whom will the day of the Lord of hosts be?
What is the counsel of 2:22?
options: Cease from man, whose breath is in his nostrils

3.title: Leadership removed
3.text: God takes away Jerusalem's stays and gives children to rule — a sign of social collapse.

3.question: Whom does the Lord say He will give as princes to Jerusalem?
What is to be said to the righteous?
options: That it shall be well with him

3.question: Of what does the Lord accuse the elders and princes?
options: Of grinding the faces of the poor

3.question: The chapter describes the removal of what from the daughters of Zion?
What will happen to Zion's gates?

4.title: The Branch of the Lord
4.text: Amid judgment, hope arises: the 'Branch' and the holy remnant of Zion.

4.question: According to 4:2, the Branch of the Lord shall be?

5.title: The song of the vineyard
5.text: God tended the vineyard, but it yielded wild grapes — a figure of Israel and its injustice.

5.question: What did the vineyard yield instead of good grapes?
According to 5:7, what is the vineyard of the Lord of hosts?
To whom is the woe of those who rise early (5:11)?
According to 5:20, against whom is the woe?
options: Those who call evil good, and good evil

5.question: What refrain marks the Lord's anger in this chapter?
options: His hand is stretched out still

5.question: To whom will the Lord hiss, lifting an ensign (5:26)?

6.title: Holy, Holy, Holy
6.text: In the year Uzziah died, Isaiah sees the Lord on the throne and is commissioned.

6.question: What does Isaiah say when he sees the Lord?
options: Woe is me, I am undone, a man of unclean lips

6.question: How does Isaiah answer 'Whom shall I send?'
To what is the tenth that remains compared (6:13)?

7.title: The sign of Immanuel
7.text: Isaiah urges King Ahaz to believe God before the threat of Syria and Israel.

7.question: How does God call the two enemy kings Ahaz feared?
According to 7:9, if you will not believe?
What sign will the Lord Himself give the house of David?
options: A virgin shall conceive and bear a son, Immanuel

7.question: Before the child knows to choose good, the land of the two kings shall be?
With what will the Lord 'shave' the head (7:20)?
options: With a hired razor (the king of Assyria)

8.title: Maher-shalal-hash-baz
8.text: The name of Isaiah's son means 'speed to the spoil' — a sign of the imminent fall of Damascus and Samaria.

8.question: What name is given to the son, a sign of the speed of the spoil?
According to 8:8, the flood (Assyria) will reach how far?
According to 8:10, why will the enemies' counsel not stand?
Instead of consulting mediums and the dead, to what should one turn (8:20)?
options: To the law and to the testimony

9.title: Unto us a child is born
9.text: Isaiah 9:6 proclaims the Messiah's names, read at Christmas worldwide.

9.question: Which of these is one of the names of the child born (9:6)?
According to 9:7, upon whose throne will His kingdom be established?
How are the leaders of this people described (9:16)?
What refrain is repeated about the Lord's anger?
options: His hand is stretched out still

10.title: The rod of mine anger
10.text: God uses Assyria to punish, but will judge its arrogance.

10.question: What does the Lord call Assyria in 10:5?
What does Assyria boast of in 10:9?
options: Having conquered cities like Calno and Carchemish

10.question: How does Assyria boast of gathering the nations' riches?
options: Like one gathering abandoned eggs in a nest

10.question: Upon whom will Israel's remnant lean again (10:20)?
options: The Lord, the Holy One of Israel

10.question: What does the Lord tell His people about Assyria (10:24)?
options: Not to fear, for His anger will soon cease

10.question: The chapter describes the enemy's advance passing cities like?
According to 10:34, what will fall by a mighty one?

11.title: The rod of Jesse
11.text: Messianic prophecy: the Branch of David's line and the kingdom of peace.

11.question: According to 11:1, from where will the rod and Branch come?
According to 11:6, who will dwell with the lamb?
What will the Lord do for the dispersed of Israel and Judah (11:12)?
options: Set up an ensign and gather them from the ends of the earth

12.title: Song of praise
12.text: A brief hymn of gratitude closes the first section of the book.

12.question: According to 12:3, from where shall water be drawn with joy?

13.title: The burden of Babylon
13.text: Opens the series of oracles against the nations.

13.question: According to 13:10, what will happen to sun, moon, and stars?
According to 13:12, what will God make more precious than fine gold?
To what will Babylon be compared in its fall (13:19)?

14.title: The fall of the proud
14.text: The proverb against the king of Babylon and the lament over the 'morning star.'

14.question: What proverb will Israel take up against the king of Babylon?
According to 14:9, whom does hell rouse to meet the one who falls?
options: The dead and the kings of nations

14.question: Who said 'I will ascend into heaven... I will be like the most High' and was cast down?
options: The morning star (son of the morning)

14.question: Why will the seed of evildoers not be named (14:20)?
options: Because they destroyed their land and slew their people

14.question: What nation will the Lord break in His own land (14:25)?
What shall be answered to the nations' messengers (14:32)?
options: That the Lord hath founded Zion

15.title: The burden of Moab
15.text: A lament for Moab's cities, laid waste by night.

15.question: Against what nation is this burden?
What will the Lord send upon those who escape from Moab (15:9)?

16.title: The throne in mercy
16.text: Amid Moab's judgment shines 16:5: a throne established in mercy.

16.question: According to 16:5, in what will the throne be established?
Within how many years will Moab's glory be brought to contempt (16:14)?

17.title: The burden of Damascus
17.text: Damascus, capital of Syria, shall cease to be a city.

17.question: According to 17:1, what will Damascus become?
According to 17:7, to whom will man look in that day?
options: To his Maker (the Holy One of Israel)

17.question: According to 17:14, what is the lot of those who spoil us?
options: At evening trouble; before morning they are not

18.title: Beyond the rivers of Cush
18.text: A brief oracle on a nation that sends ambassadors; God watches in silence.

18.question: According to 18:7, where shall a present be brought to the Lord of hosts?

19.title: The burden of Egypt
19.text: Judgment on Egypt that ends in a vision of conversion.

19.question: How does the Lord come to Egypt (19:1)?
According to 19:2, against whom will God set the Egyptians?
options: Against the Egyptians themselves (city against city)

19.question: What did the Lord mingle in the midst of Egypt (19:14)?
According to 19:20, what will God send to Egypt when they cry out?
options: A Saviour and a great one (a Redeemer)

19.question: How is Israel called in the final blessing (19:25)?

20.title: Naked and barefoot
20.text: Isaiah enacts a sign against trusting in Egypt and Ethiopia.

20.question: For how many years did Isaiah walk naked and barefoot as a sign (20:3)?

21.title: Babylon is fallen
21.text: A watchman keeps guard until he announces Babylon's fall.

21.question: In 21:2, who is called to lay siege?
What does the watchman finally announce (21:9)?
Against which region is the burden of 21:13?
According to 21:16, within a year the glory of Kedar will be?

22.title: The valley of vision
22.text: Jerusalem feasts before judgment; Shebna is deposed and Eliakim exalted.

22.question: About what place is the burden of 22:1?
In making the reservoir, to whom did the people not look (22:11)?
options: To Him who made it (who fashioned it)

22.question: What did the people say instead of weeping (22:13)?
options: 'Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die'

22.question: Whom will the Lord call in Shebna's place (22:20)?
What will be laid on Eliakim's shoulder (22:22)?
options: The key of the house of David

23.title: The burden of Tyre
23.text: Tyre was the wealthy Phoenician port, 'the crowning city,' whose merchants were princes.

23.question: Against what city is this burden?
Who purposed it against Tyre, to stain its pride?
What people is mentioned in 23:13, whom Assyria founded for the wilderness?
For how many years will Tyre be forgotten (23:15)?

24.title: Isaiah's apocalypse
24.text: Chs. 24-27 describe a cosmic judgment upon the whole earth.

24.question: Why is the earth defiled (24:5)?
options: Because they broke the everlasting covenant

24.question: According to 24:1, what does the Lord do to the earth?
What does 24:15 say to do to the Lord in the valleys and isles?
According to 24:20, the earth shall reel to and fro like?
What happens to the sun and moon when the Lord reigns in Zion (24:23)?

25.title: A song of praise
25.text: Gratitude for God's protection and the promise of victory over death.

25.question: According to 25:4, to whom was the Lord a strength?
options: To the poor and the needy

25.question: According to 25:8, what will the Lord do with death and tears?
options: Swallow up death in victory and wipe away tears

25.question: According to 25:6, what will the Lord make on this mountain for all peoples?

26.title: A song of trust
26.text: In Judah they sing of trust in the Lord, the everlasting rock, and the hope of resurrection.

26.question: According to 26:3, whom does the Lord keep in peace?
options: Him whose mind is stayed on Him

26.question: According to 26:10, what does the wicked not learn, even when shown favor?
According to 26:13, what does the people remember, despite other lords?
What does Isaiah affirm about the dead in 26:19?
options: Thy dead men shall live (rise again)

27.title: The Lord's vineyard
27.text: God keeps His vineyard and gathers the scattered at the sound of a great trumpet.

27.question: What creature will the Lord punish with His sword (27:1)?
According to 27:6, what will Jacob and Israel do?
options: Take root and fill the world with fruit

27.question: According to 27:13, at the great trumpet, what will the scattered do?

28.title: Precept upon precept
28.text: Woe to the drunkards of Ephraim; instruction comes little by little, and God lays the corner stone in Zion.

28.question: The woe of 28:1 is to whose crown of pride?
According to 28:7, why do even the priest and prophet err?
How does instruction come, according to 28:10?
options: Precept upon precept, line upon line

28.question: What stone does the Lord lay in Zion as a foundation (28:16)?
options: A precious corner stone, a sure foundation

28.question: The parable of 28:24-29 is about whom, to show God's wisdom?
From whom does the plowman's wisdom come (28:29)?

29.title: Woe to Ariel
29.text: Ariel is Jerusalem, the city where David camped; it will be brought low and then restored.

29.question: What did the Lord pour out upon them (29:10)?
According to 29:13, how does this people honor God?
options: With mouth and lips, but heart far away

29.question: In 29:16, the people's perversity is compared to what?
options: The potter treated as the clay (the work denying its maker)

29.question: According to 29:18, what will the deaf and blind do in that day?

30.title: Trusting in Egypt
30.text: Woe to the rebels who seek Egypt; but in quietness and confidence is strength.

30.question: To whom do the rebellious children go down for strength (30:2)?
What does the people ask of the prophets (30:10)?
According to 30:15, in confidence and quietness would be strength, but the people?
What voice will be heard behind them (30:21)?
options: 'This is the way, walk ye in it'

30.question: According to 30:26, how will the light of the sun be in that day?
According to 30:29, there will be a song as in the night when one?
According to 30:31, by the voice of the Lord, who shall be beaten down?

31.title: Egypt is man, not God
31.text: Trusting in Egypt's horses and chariots is vain; the Lord will defend Jerusalem.

31.question: Woe to those who go down to Egypt and trust in (31:1)?
According to 31:8, by what sword shall Assyria fall?

32.title: A King shall reign in righteousness
32.text: Vision of the righteous King and the peace that comes when the spirit is poured from on high.

32.question: According to 32:1, how shall the King reign?
Whom does Isaiah call to listen in 32:9?
options: The women at ease and careless

32.question: According to 32:15, what will turn the wilderness into a fruitful field?
options: The spirit poured from on high

32.question: According to 32:17, what shall be the work of righteousness?

33.title: The King in His beauty
33.text: Woe to the spoiler; the righteous shall dwell on high and see the King.

33.question: To whom is the woe of 33:1?
What does the Lord say He will do 'now' (33:10)?
options: Arise, be exalted, and lift Himself up

33.question: According to 33:15-16, who shall dwell on high, with sure bread and water?
According to 33:20, Zion is described as a tabernacle that?
According to 33:24, the people who dwell there shall be?

34.title: The judgment of Edom
34.text: The Lord's sword descends on Edom; the land will lie desolate forever.

34.question: Upon what people will the Lord's sword descend for judgment (34:5)?
What birds will possess Edom's desolate land (34:11)?
Where should one seek and read that none of this shall fail (34:16)?
options: In the book of the Lord

35.title: The desert shall blossom
35.text: A chapter of hope: healing, joy, and the holy way back to Zion.

35.question: According to 35:1, what will the wilderness do?
options: Be glad and blossom as the rose

35.question: What will the highway of 35:8 be called?

36.title: Rabshakeh defies Jerusalem
36.text: The Assyrian envoy mocks Judah's trust in God and in Egypt.

36.question: Who came up against Judah and sent Rabshakeh (36:1-2)?
In what 'broken reed' does Rabshakeh say Judah trusts (36:6)?
In what language did Rabshakeh cry out loudly (36:13)?
What does Rabshakeh mock, saying the gods of the nations could not do (36:18)?
options: Deliver their lands from the king of Assyria

36.question: Why did the people not answer Rabshakeh (36:21)?
options: The king had commanded: answer him not

37.title: Hezekiah's prayer
37.text: Hezekiah prays, and the angel of the Lord delivers Jerusalem miraculously.

37.question: What did Hezekiah do on hearing Rabshakeh's words (37:1)?
options: Rent his clothes, put on sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord

37.question: What does Sennacherib's letter try to make Hezekiah believe (37:10)?
options: That his God would not deliver Jerusalem

37.question: In his prayer, Hezekiah declares who alone is God of all kingdoms (37:16)?
How does Isaiah say the daughter of Zion reacts to the Assyrian king (37:22)?
options: Despises him and laughs him to scorn

37.question: What did Sennacherib boast of, defying the Lord (37:24)?
options: Climbing the mountains and cutting Lebanon's cedars

37.question: The Lord promises the Assyrian king will not do what to the city (37:33)?
options: Will not enter it nor shoot an arrow

37.question: How many did the angel of the Lord smite in the Assyrian camp (37:36)?

38.title: Fifteen more years
38.text: Hezekiah falls mortally ill, prays, and the Lord prolongs his life.

38.question: How many years did the Lord add to Hezekiah's life (38:5)?
What sign did the Lord give, on the sundial (degrees) of Ahaz (38:8)?
options: The shadow went back ten degrees

38.question: What did the Lord cast behind His back (38:17)?
What poultice did Isaiah order laid on the boil (38:21)?

39.title: The envoys of Babylon
39.text: Hezekiah shows his treasures, and Isaiah foretells the Babylonian exile.

39.question: What did Hezekiah show the messengers of Babylon (39:2)?
Where does Isaiah prophesy all will be carried (39:6)?

40.title: Comfort ye my people
40.text: Opens the second part of Isaiah, of comfort and redemption.

40.question: What does the voice in the wilderness cry (40:3)?
options: 'Prepare ye the way of the Lord'

40.question: Before God, the nations are as (40:15)?
According to 40:18, to whom can God be likened?
What will they that wait upon the Lord do (40:31)?
options: Renew their strength and mount up with wings as eagles

41.title: Fear not, I am with thee
41.text: God comforts Israel, His servant, and exposes the vanity of idols.

41.question: Who is the first and with the last, calling the generations (41:4)?
What does God say to Israel in 41:10?
options: 'Fear not, for I am with thee'

41.question: How does God call Israel as He comforts it (41:14)?
What will God do in the wilderness (41:18)?
From where does God raise the one who will come (41:25)?
options: The north (the rising of the sun)

41.question: According to 41:29, what are the molten images?

42.title: The song of the Servant
42.text: The first of the four 'Servant Songs,' read as prophecy of Christ.

42.question: What will the Servant not do to the bruised reed or smoking flax (42:3)?
options: Break the reed or quench the flax

42.question: For what did God give the Servant (42:6)?
options: For a covenant of the people and a light to the gentiles

42.question: According to 42:8, to whom will the Lord not give His glory?
options: To no one; nor His praise to graven images

42.question: According to 42:16, God will turn the darkness before the blind into?
How is the people described in 42:22?

43.title: I have redeemed thee
43.text: Promises of presence and forgiveness: God calls Israel by name.

43.question: According to 43:2, passing through the fire, the people shall not be?
How does God call Israel in 43:10?
For Israel's sake, against whom did God send enemies (43:14)?
What will God do anew (43:19)?
options: A new thing: a way in the desert, rivers in the wilderness

43.question: For what did God form this people (43:21)?
According to 43:25, what does God do with transgressions and sins?
options: Blots out transgressions and remembers sins no more

44.title: Cyrus, the shepherd
44.text: God mocks idols and names Cyrus, who will rebuild Jerusalem.

44.question: What will God pour upon Jacob's seed (44:3)?
According to 44:9, what are the makers of idols?
According to 44:13, the carpenter makes the image after the likeness of?
In the satire of 44:16-17, a man burns half the wood and of the rest makes?
According to 44:22, God blots out transgressions like?
Whom does the Lord call 'my shepherd,' who will rebuild Jerusalem (44:28)?

45.title: Cyrus, the anointed
45.text: God names Cyrus ~150 years before his reign — proof that He alone foretells the future.

45.question: Whom does the Lord call 'his anointed' (45:1)?
In 45:9, to whom is the woe, like clay striving with the potter?
options: Him who strives with his Maker

45.question: How does Isaiah call God in 45:15?
options: A God that hidest thyself, the Saviour

45.question: According to 45:18, why did God create the earth?
What does God invite all the ends of the earth to do (45:22)?
options: Look unto Him and be saved

46.title: Bel and Nebo
46.text: Babylon's idols must be carried; Israel's God is the one who carries His people.

46.question: What idols of Babylon are mentioned, bowing down (46:1)?
What does God declare from the beginning (46:10)?
Whom does God call a 'ravenous bird from the east' (46:11)?

47.title: The fall of Babylon
47.text: The 'virgin daughter of the Chaldees' will come down from her throne to the dust.

47.question: To whom is it said 'come down, sit in the dust' (47:1)?
options: The daughter of Babylon (of the Chaldees)

47.question: What did Babylon say in her heart (47:8,10)?
options: 'I am, and none else beside me'

47.question: Whom does Babylon call, in vain, to save her (47:13)?

48.title: Go forth from Babylon
48.text: God announced everything beforehand so Israel would not credit the idols.

48.question: Why did God announce things before they happened (48:5)?
options: So they would not attribute them to idols

48.question: Where does God say He refined/chose Israel (48:10)?
According to 48:18, had Israel hearkened to the commandments, its peace would be like?
According to 48:22, what is there not for the wicked?

49.title: A light to the gentiles
49.text: The second Servant Song: he is given as a light to the gentiles and salvation to the ends of the earth.

49.question: From when did the Lord call the servant (49:1)?
According to 49:6, the servant is given as a light to the?
According to 49:15, though a mother forget her child, God?
According to 49:16, where does God say He has graven Zion?
options: Upon the palms of His hands

49.question: According to 49:26, what shall all flesh know?
options: That the Lord is the Saviour and Redeemer

50.title: The obedient servant
50.text: The third Servant Song: he suffers insult, trusting in God's help.

50.question: The servant gave his back to the smiters and his face to those who (50:6)?
He who walks in darkness with no light should trust in what (50:10)?

51.title: Look to Abraham
51.text: God will comfort Zion and make her desert like Eden.

51.question: To whom should one look, as a father, in 51:2?
What did God do to the sea, that the redeemed might pass over (51:10)?
options: Dried the sea and made a way

51.question: According to 51:6, the heavens shall vanish like smoke, but God's salvation?
What cup did Jerusalem drink from the Lord's hand (51:17)?

52.title: Thy God reigneth
52.text: Zion awakes to deliverance; the fourth Servant Song begins.

52.question: What is Zion called to do (52:1)?
What is beautiful upon the mountains (52:7)?
options: The feet of him who brings good tidings (peace)

52.question: According to 52:13-14, the servant's visage was?

53.title: The suffering Servant
53.text: The most detailed Old Testament prophecy of Christ's atonement.

53.question: Why was the servant wounded and bruised (53:5)?
How was the servant led, without opening his mouth (53:7)?
options: As a lamb to the slaughter

54.title: No weapon shall prosper
54.text: Promises of everlasting kindness and protection to Zion.

54.question: Who is the husband and Redeemer of Zion (54:5)?
Though the mountains move, what shall not depart (54:10)?
What promise does God make about weapons formed against Zion (54:17)?

55.title: Come to the waters
55.text: A gracious invitation and the promise of God's effectual word.

55.question: To whom is the invitation to come to the waters and buy without money (55:1)?
How are God's ways and thoughts, compared to ours (55:8-9)?
options: Higher (as the heavens above the earth)

55.question: According to 55:11, the word that goes from God's mouth shall not return?

56.title: A house of prayer
56.text: Eunuchs and strangers who join themselves to the Lord are welcomed.

56.question: Blessed is he who keeps from profaning what (56:2)?
What shall God's house be called (56:7)?
options: A house of prayer for all people

57.title: The High One and the contrite
57.text: God dwells on high and also with the contrite in spirit.

57.question: What happens to the righteous, with none considering it (57:1)?
According to 57:13, who puts his trust in God shall possess?
With whom does the High and Lofty One dwell, besides on high (57:15)?
options: With the contrite and humble in spirit

57.question: What does my God say about the wicked (57:21)?

58.title: The true fast
58.text: God prefers justice and mercy to empty ritual.

58.question: According to 58:5, is the true fast merely to?
options: Afflict the soul for a day (bow the head as a bulrush)

58.question: What fast does God choose (58:6-7)?
options: To loose the bands of wickedness and share bread with the hungry

58.question: He who honors the sabbath, delighting in it, shall delight in what (58:13-14)?

59.title: Iniquities separate
59.text: It is not God's hand that is short; sins separate the people from Him.

59.question: According to 59:2, what separates the people from God?
According to 59:1, is the Lord's hand too short to save?
According to 59:14, what stumbles in the streets?
Who shall come to Zion and to those who turn from transgression (59:20)?

60.title: Arise, shine
60.text: The glory of the Lord rises upon Zion, and the nations come to her light.

60.question: What is Zion called to do in 60:1?
options: Arise and shine (thy light is come)

60.question: Who shall walk in Zion's light (60:3)?
options: The nations (and kings to the brightness of her rising)

60.question: What shall Zion call her walls and gates (60:18)?
Who shall be Zion's everlasting light, in place of the sun (60:19)?

61.title: The Spirit of the Lord upon me
61.text: Jesus read this text in Nazareth and said: 'Today it is fulfilled' (Luke 4).

61.question: Why was the anointed sent (61:1)?
options: To preach good tidings to the meek and liberty to the captives

61.question: What shall be given to Zion's mourners, in place of ashes (61:3)?
options: Beauty (oil of joy, garment of praise)

62.title: A new name
62.text: Zion will receive a new name and be called 'Sought out, a city not forsaken.'

62.question: How will God rejoice over Zion (62:5)?
options: As the bridegroom rejoices over the bride

62.question: How shall Zion's people be called (62:12)?
options: The holy people, the redeemed of the Lord

63.title: Who comes from Edom
63.text: The image of him who trod the winepress of wrath alone, and the memory of God's mercies.

63.question: What does he who comes from Edom say he did alone (63:3)?
Whom did the people remember, from the days of old (63:11)?
Who saved the people in all their affliction (63:9)?
options: The angel of His presence (God redeemed them)

63.question: Whom does the people acknowledge as Father and Redeemer (63:16)?

64.title: We the clay, thou the potter
64.text: A prayer of humility and the people's repentance.

64.question: What does the prophet ask God to do (64:1)?
options: Rend the heavens and come down

64.question: The relationship between God and the people is like (64:8)?
According to 64:6, our righteousnesses are as?

65.title: New heavens and a new earth
65.text: God separates His servants from the rebels and promises a new creation.

65.question: Why did God destine the rebels to the sword (65:12)?
options: Because He called and they answered not, and did evil

65.question: What does God say He will create (65:17)?
options: New heavens and a new earth

65.question: In the new time, the people shall build houses and (65:21)?
options: Inhabit them (and eat the fruit of vineyards)

65.question: Who shall feed together with the lamb on the holy mount (65:25)?

66.title: Heaven is my throne
66.text: The book closes with universal worship and the warning of judgment.

66.question: What are heaven and earth to God (66:1)?
What unheard-of thing does 66:8 describe about a nation?
options: That it can be born at once (Zion brought forth)

66.question: With what will the Lord come to judgment with all flesh (66:15-16)?
What will God do with all nations and tongues (66:18)?
options: Gather them to see His glory

66.question: From new moon to new moon and sabbath to sabbath, all flesh shall come to (66:23)?
